sections.name: Opening & Rapport
sections.description: How well the agent opened the call and built initial rapport

questions.question_text: Did the agent introduce themselves and their company clearly?

answer_definitions.Yes: Agent stated their name and company name within the first 15 seconds of the call.
answer_definitions.No: Agent did not state their name or company name, or the introduction was unclear or missing.

questions.question_text: Rate the agent's ability to build rapport and establish a conversational tone.

answer_definitions.0: Agent was robotic, scripted, or made no effort to connect with the prospect. No small talk, personalization, or warmth.
answer_definitions.1: Agent attempted rapport but it felt surface-level or forced. Generic pleasantries without genuine engagement or personalization.
answer_definitions.2: Agent naturally built rapport through personalization, relevant small talk, or referencing something specific to the prospect's situation. The tone felt conversational, not transactional.

sections.name: Discovery & Needs Assessment
sections.description: How effectively the agent uncovered the prospect's needs and pain points

questions.question_text: Did the agent ask open-ended discovery questions to understand the prospect's current situation?

answer_definitions.Yes: Agent asked at least two open-ended questions (not yes/no) to explore the prospect's current workflow, challenges, or goals. Examples: 'What does your current process look like?', 'What's driving the decision to evaluate new solutions?'
answer_definitions.No: Agent jumped straight to pitching without asking about the prospect's situation, or only asked closed yes/no questions.

questions.question_text: Rate how well the agent identified and articulated the prospect's pain points.

answer_definitions.0: Agent failed to identify any specific pain points. The conversation stayed at a surface level with no understanding of what the prospect is struggling with.
answer_definitions.1: Agent identified at least one pain point but did not dig deeper to understand its impact on the business or quantify the problem.
answer_definitions.2: Agent identified specific pain points and explored their business impact through follow-up questions. The agent demonstrated genuine understanding of why the prospect needs a solution.

questions.question_text: Did the agent confirm they understood the prospect's needs before presenting the solution?

answer_definitions.Yes: Agent summarized or restated the prospect's needs, challenges, or goals before transitioning to the pitch. Example: 'So it sounds like the main issue is X and you're looking for Y — is that right?'
answer_definitions.No: Agent transitioned directly from discovery to pitching without confirming understanding, or skipped discovery entirely.

sections.name: Product Presentation & Value Proposition
sections.description: How effectively the agent presented the product and tied it to prospect needs

questions.question_text: Rate how well the agent tailored the product pitch to the prospect's specific needs.

answer_definitions.0: Agent delivered a generic, one-size-fits-all pitch with no connection to the prospect's stated needs or pain points. Focused purely on features without relevance.
answer_definitions.1: Agent mentioned some relevant features but the pitch was mostly generic. Some connection to the prospect's needs, but it felt like a standard script with minor adjustments.
answer_definitions.2: Agent clearly tied specific product capabilities to the prospect's stated pain points. Used the prospect's own language and examples from the discovery phase to demonstrate value.

questions.question_text: Did the agent provide accurate product information? (Mark 'No' only if the agent made a verifiably false claim.)

answer_definitions.Yes: All product claims made by the agent appear accurate based on the transcript context. No demonstrably false statements about features, pricing, or capabilities.
answer_definitions.No: Agent made at least one factually incorrect statement about the product — such as overpromising a feature that doesn't exist, quoting wrong pricing, or misrepresenting capabilities.

sections.name: Objection Handling
sections.description: How the agent responded to prospect objections or concerns

questions.question_text: Rate the agent's ability to handle objections raised by the prospect.

answer_definitions.0: Agent ignored objections, became defensive, or dismissed the prospect's concerns. No attempt to address the underlying issue. Or no objection was raised (mark N/A if na_eligible).
answer_definitions.1: Agent acknowledged the objection but the response was weak, generic, or unconvincing. Did not fully resolve the concern or provide supporting evidence.
answer_definitions.2: Agent acknowledged the objection empathetically, addressed the underlying concern with a specific and credible response (e.g., case study, data point, alternative approach), and confirmed the prospect was satisfied with the answer.

sections.name: Closing & Next Steps
sections.description: How the agent closed the call and secured a commitment

questions.question_text: Did the agent propose a clear next step or call-to-action?

answer_definitions.Yes: Agent proposed a specific next step with a timeframe — such as scheduling a demo, sending a proposal, setting up a trial, or booking a follow-up call with a specific date/time.
answer_definitions.No: Agent ended the call without proposing a next step, or the next step was vague (e.g., 'I'll send you some info' without specifying what or when).

questions.question_text: Did the agent secure a commitment from the prospect for the next step?

answer_definitions.Yes: The prospect verbally agreed to a next step — such as confirming a meeting time, agreeing to a demo, or committing to review a proposal by a specific date.
answer_definitions.No: The call ended without a firm commitment from the prospect. The agent either didn't ask or the prospect left it ambiguous (e.g., 'maybe', 'I'll think about it') without the agent attempting to pin down specifics.

questions.question_text: Did the agent confirm the prospect's contact details or preferred communication channel?

answer_definitions.Yes: Agent confirmed or asked for the prospect's email, phone number, or preferred channel for follow-up communication.
answer_definitions.No: Agent did not confirm contact details or communication preferences before ending the call.
